spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 import with JUnit 5 import for the @Test annotation.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: org.junit.Test is not available in JUnit 5; tests will fail to compile if JUnit 4 dependencies are removed or if the project enforces JUnit 5 platform.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 @RunWith annotation with JUnit 5 @ExtendWith annotation.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: @RunWith is a JUnit 4 specific annotation and is ignored or causes errors in JUnit 5 platform; Spring Boot 3 requires JUnit 5 extensions.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 SpringRunner import with JUnit 5 SpringExtension import.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: SpringRunner is part of the JUnit 4 integration; SpringExtension is the JUnit 5 equivalent required for Spring Boot 3.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Migrate JUnit 4 imports to JUnit 5 (Jupiter) imports.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: JUnit 4 classes (org.junit.Test, org.junit.Assert) are not part of the JUnit 5 API; tests will fail to compile or run if JUnit 5 is the active test engine without these imports.
Spring Boot 3 requires JUnit 5. The @RunWith annotation is JUnit 4 specific and ignored by the JUnit 5 engine, causing the Spring context not to load and tests to fail.
The 'secure' attribute was removed in Spring Security 6 (used by Spring Boot 3). Keeping it causes a compilation error.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Add @WithMockUser to handle Spring Security 6 default strict security.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: Spring Security 6 enables strict security by default. Without @WithMockUser (or disabling security), requests in tests will be rejected with 401/403, causing test assertions to fail.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/main/java/.general_idea: Prefix in-memory passwords with {noop} to satisfy Spring Security 5.8+ requirement for password encoding identifiers.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/main/java/.why_exists: Spring Security 5.8+ throws an IllegalArgumentException at runtime if a password is provided without an encoding identifier (like {noop}, {bcrypt}, etc.) when a PasswordEncoder bean is present.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Update import for LocalServerPort to match Spring Boot 3 package structure.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: The class org.springframework.boot.context.embedded.LocalServerPort was removed in Spring Boot 3; using the old import causes a compile error.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Add explicit throws declaration for JSONException to satisfy compiler requirements.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: JSONAssert.assertEquals throws JSONException; without the throws clause, the code fails to compile.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Add import for JSONException to support the new throws declaration.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: The JSONException class is not imported by default; without this import, the throws clause causes a compile error.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Remove deprecated 'secure' attribute from @WebMvcTest annotation to comply with Spring Boot 3.0 API changes.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: The 'secure' attribute was removed from @WebMvcTest in Spring Boot 3.0; retaining it causes a compilation error.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Add @WithMockUser annotation to handle security context requirements in Spring Boot 3.0 tests.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.0 enforces stricter security defaults; without a mock user, requests may fail with 401/403 or security context initialization errors.

spring-boot-web-application/src/main/jav.general_idea: Add a NoOpPasswordEncoder to in-memory authentication configuration to satisfy Spring Security 5.8+ requirement for explicit password encoding.
spring-boot-web-application/src/main/jav.why_exists: Spring Security 5.8+ (used in Spring Boot 3) throws an IllegalArgumentException at runtime if no PasswordEncoder is configured for in-memory authentication, as it no longer allows plain text passwords by default.
